stories.headline: Feeding PDFs directly to vision models costs more and answers worse
stories.summary: The 'just attach the PDF' shortcut underperforms. A single-author benchmark across 171 questions on image-heavy documents found native vision models scored 52% accuracy at $0.26 per query, while extraction pipelines hit nearly 60% for less. Single Reddit thread, no peer review. Before building document question-answering on vision alone, run this test against your own corpus.

sections.name: hands_on

stories.headline: llama.cpp now runs Qwen3.5 with better precision on consumer hardware
stories.summary: llama.cpp build b9297 adds NVFP4 multi-token-prediction scale tensors and links Qwen3.5 multi-token-prediction tensors, improving quantised inference fidelity for those models locally. This is a plumbing update, not a capability leap. If you run Qwen3.5 on local hardware, pull the latest build before benchmarking; otherwise, nothing urgent here.

stories.headline: Local inference on Intel hardware gains support for a newer model architecture
stories.summary: llama.cpp build b9289 extends its Intel SYCL backend to support gated delta networks with sequence lengths greater than one, enabling a broader class of recurrent-style models to run locally on Intel GPUs. Narrow but practical: if you run local inference on Intel silicon, pull this build before testing recurrent architectures.

sections.intro_body: Today's updates are maintenance, not milestones. The pattern is narrow backend fixes that quietly expand which hardware can run which models locally, with no new capabilities attached.
